Thank you for your consideration and help.",0,tobiasbuek,"Inquiry on Cybersecurity Protocols for Medical Data Security

Problem with Google Analytics,"Hello Customer Support, I am contacting you regarding an issue we are facing with our website's analytics. The tracking has ceased to function, and we are unable to gather data on our website's traffic. We believe the problem might be due to an incorrect configuration of our Google Tag. We have verified the tag codes and reviewed the documentation, but we are unable to fix it. We would be very grateful for your assistance in diagnosing and resolving the issue. Could you provide us with instructions on how to correctly set up our Google Tag or suggest other methods to get our analytics tracking operational? We are eager to hear from you and resolve this issue as quickly as possible. Thank you for your time and support.",0,tobiasbuek,"Problem with Google Analytics

Hello Customer Support, I am contacting you regarding an issue we are facing with our website's analytics. We have verified the tag codes and reviewed the documentation, but we are unable to fix it. We would be very grateful for your assistance in diagnosing and resolving the issue. Could you provide us with instructions on how to correctly set up our Google Tag or suggest other methods to get our analytics tracking operational? We are eager to hear from you and resolve this issue as quickly as possible. Thank you for your time and support."

Customer Support Ticket,"The data analytics platform has experienced a crash, and the Alteryx workflow failed to load due to possible insufficient RAM or Linux configuration issues. After restarting the server and reviewing the logs, the problem still exists. Can you please supply more detail on the error messages and the specific logs you examined? Also, could you provide information on the current RAM allocation and Linux configuration settings? These details will aid in diagnosing whether the issue is due to insufficient RAM or a Linux configuration problem. If further investigation is needed, I may need to arrange a call with you. Sticky summer for inflation,"Plus, China’s demographic decline and Hillary Clinton in conversation ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ All newsletters | Read in browser Roula Khalaf Editor ‌ ‌ May 25 2023 Hello from London, where the newsroom has been reporting on rather bad economic news: UK inflation fell to 8.7 per cent in April, according to the Office for National Statistics — a much smaller drop than the Bank of England expected. UK bond markets jolted at the news, with gilt yields nearing the levels of last year’s “mini” Budget crisis. Inflation in the UK is now about double the equivalent US rate, and well above almost all of western Europe. The return of high inflation and slow growth is reminiscent of the 1970s. The FT’s economics editor Chris Giles is therefore asking: Is the UK once again the ‘sick man’ of Europe?Traders predict more trouble is ahead for UK bonds, after stronger than expected inflation data yesterday © Reuters My choices this week ‌ ‌ US legislators have been drawing up rules that affect their personal investments because of antiquated loopholes in conflict of interest laws. This excellent FT analysis exposes how lawmakers could be benefiting from Capitol Hill’s failure to keep pace with a transformed investment sector.Russia is fortifying its frontline in anticipation of the long-awaited Ukrainian counter-offensive. By analysing satellite imagery and mapping changes on the ground, our correspondent in Kyiv explores how the attack might play out. (free to read)“Forever chemicals” that are vital to the production of microchips could have life-threatening health and environmental effects, growing evidence suggests. Facing calls for more stringent regulation, how is a rapidly growing industry responding to the threat?(If you’d like to read this article for free, please download our iPhone app, FT Edit, where it is in today’s edition. You don’t need to register or give payment details, you can simply download and start reading FT Edit, and the first month is free.)The G7 heads of government painted a picture of unipolar economic dominance at their summit in Hiroshima last weekend. But despite its best efforts, the G7 cannot run the world, writes the FT’s chief economics commentator Martin Wolf.China’s rapidly ageing population — in part a legacy of its one-child policy — is forcing the government to make painful reforms tackling pensions, healthcare and the workforce. This poignant report from Rudong, China’s oldest county, offers a snapshot of China’s demographic decline. (free to read)Last weekend I was in Washington DC for a brilliant day at the FT Weekend festival. Among other highlights, the 2016 Democratic presidential candidate Hillary Clinton discussed China, Putin, Trump and Netflix series The Diplomat, with the FT’s US national editor Ed Luce. Watch the interview on ft.com here.Thanks for readingRoula PS Don’t forget our special subscription offer, which means you get a third off a Standard digital subscription to the Financial Times. This gives you access to ft.com as well as excellent newsletters including the award-winning Inside Politics, Working It and our flagship news briefing, FirstFT. ‌ ‌ Recommended newsletters for you In Today’s FT Your daily overview of FT’s top stories. column1: Say Hello to Clearer Nights on the Road,"Finally, a Glare Solution for Drivers Make night driving safer and clearer with ClearView Most accidents happen in low light — and glare is a big reason why. ClearView night vision glasses are engineered to block headlight scatter and help you drive confidently in any condition. Whether it's dusk, or you're dealing with oncoming traffic, these anti-glare glasses can make a huge difference. Lightweight, stylish, and built for performance. Style + Function = ClearView No more flimsy gas station shades. ClearView is made with premium materials that won’t break when you toss them in the glove box. Spring-loaded hinges, soft silicone nose pads, and a universal fit for men and women make them a great everyday driver’s choice. ? LIMITED STOCK — Order Now!
